assertions.expectation: Recommends Stable or Extended release channel to minimize upgrade frequency
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends Extended release channel and explains it provides 24-month support period vs 14-month standard, with no automatic minor version upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' maintenance exclusion to block disruptive upgrades while allowing security patches on the control plane
assertions.reason: Response specifically recommends --add-maintenance-exclusion-scope no_minor_or_node_upgrades and explains it blocks control plane minor upgrades and node pool upgrades while allowing control plane security patches

assertions.expectation: Recommends a narrow maintenance window (e.g., weekends only) so CP patches happen at predictable times
assertions.reason: Response configures Sunday 2-6 AM maintenance window with --maintenance-window-start and --maintenance-window-recurrence "FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=SU"

assertions.expectation: Suggests isolating training workloads on dedicated GPU node pools separate from serving/infrastructure pools
assertions.reason: Response creates separate node pools for training vs inference with different taints and labels, explicitly stating 'Create separate node pools for training vs inference to enable independent upgrade control'

assertions.expectation: Recommends disabling auto-upgrade on GPU training node pools and upgrading manually during scheduled gaps between training runs
assertions.reason: Response shows manual upgrade workflow for node pools and discusses timing upgrades between training campaigns, though auto-upgrade is enabled but controlled through maintenance exclusions

assertions.expectation: Advises configuring training frameworks with checkpointing so jobs can resume after any disruption
assertions.reason: Response includes specific training job manifest with CHECKPOINT_INTERVAL environment variable, terminationGracePeriodSeconds for checkpoint time, and mentions checkpoint/resume functionality validation

assertions.expectation: Mentions GPU driver version testing — always validate in staging before applying to production GPU pools
assertions.reason: Response includes a dedicated 'GPU Driver Compatibility Validation' section with specific commands for creating staging pools and testing GPU driver compatibility before production upgrades

assertions.expectation: Notes that GPUDirect/RDMA networking has strict GKE version requirements — verify compatibility before upgrading
assertions.reason: Response does not mention GPUDirect or RDMA networking requirements or their version compatibility constraints
